relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The three friends stopped answering because Job remained righteous in his own eyes.
Elihu’s anger burned because he thought Job had justified himself over the Divine.
He was also angry with the friends, who condemned Job without finding an answer.
Elihu had waited because the others were older.
When no answer appeared, his anger rose.
Young Elihu began cautiously before the aged.
He had expected years to speak wisdom.
But he now says understanding comes from spirit and the breath of Shaddai.
Great age does not guarantee justice-wisdom.
So Elihu asks to be heard.
He says he listened while the others searched for words.
He found that none had truly answered Job.
He warns them not to claim wisdom too quickly.
Job has not argued directly against Elihu, so Elihu will answer differently.
The friends have gone silent.
Elihu will not keep waiting forever.
He will speak his share.
Words press within him.
His inner pressure is like new wine in wineskins.
Speaking becomes release.
He promises not to flatter or show partiality.
He fears his Maker too much to flatter.
